subject: Chemistry
solution:
BeCl2 is dimeric in vapour phase. The coordination number of beryllium is 3.

BeCl2 is monomeric at high temperature. The coordination number of beryllium is 2.

BeCl2 is polymeric in solid state. The coordination number of beryllium is 4.

subject: Chemistry
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
solution:
The moist air becomes dry in presence of silica gel because the water molecules present in air get adsorbed on the surface of the gel. Silica gel adsorbs moisture from air via adsorption, thus protects the instrument from water corrosion (rusting) and/or prevents malfunctioning.

So, both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.

solution: For a good quality cement the ratio of silica (<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SiO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>) and alumina (<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Al</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math>) should be between <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>to</mi><mo> </mo><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. Similarly, the ratio of lime (<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CaO</mi></math>) to the total oxide mixtures consisting of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SiO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>Al</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Fe</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> should be roughly <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, If lime is in excess, the cement cracks during setting.

2BF3+6NaH→B2H6A+6NaF

In this reaction hydride ions replace the fluoride ions. and diborane is formed, which is a Lewis acid due to incomplete octet. It breaks symmetrically with bases like tertiary amines.

In this product geometry around Boron will be tetrahedral. And it will have sp3 hybridisation.

subject: Chemistry
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
Statement I:
Aluminium reacts with aqueous sodium hydroxide to form sodium tetrahydroxoaluminate(III) and liberates hydrogen gas. The reaction is given by:
$2Al + 2NaOH + 6H_2O \rightarrow 2Na^+[Al(OH)_4]^- + 3H_2$
The complex ion formed is $[Al(OH)_4]^-$, not $[Al(OH)_6]^{3-}$. Thus, Statement I is false.

Statement II:
For $ICl_4^-$, the central iodine atom has $7$ valence electrons $+ 1$ (from negative charge) $= 8$ electrons. It forms $4$ sigma bonds with Cl atoms and has $2$ lone pairs. The hybridization is $sp^3d^2$ and the shape is square planar.
For $ClO_3^-$, the central chlorine atom has $7$ valence electrons $+ 1 = 8$ electrons. It forms $3$ sigma bonds with O atoms and has $1$ lone pair. The hybridization is $sp^3$ and the shape is trigonal pyramidal.
For $IBr_2^-$, the central iodine atom has $7$ valence electrons $+ 1 = 8$ electrons. It forms $2$ sigma bonds with Br atoms and has $3$ lone pairs. The hybridization is $sp^3d$ and the shape is linear.
Thus, Statement II is true.

Therefore, Statement I is false but Statement II is true.

Answer: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The strength of the acidic character of boron trihalides depends upon π-π back bonding. In boron trihalides, π-π -back bonding occurs due to empty orbital of boron and filled orbitals of halogens.

 

The π-π back bonding is shown maximum byBF3, as the size of B and F are small and comparatively same. Due to this effect tendency of accepting lone-pair of electrons of boron decreases as the size of halogen increases. The order of size of halogens are F < Cl < Br < I . Thus, acidic nature is in order BF3<BCl3<BBr3<Bl3.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
B2H6 have four 2c-2e bonds and two 3c-2e bonds. Bridging bonds have larger bond length than terminal bonds. Angle between terminal bonds is more than angle between bridging bonds if all 4 terminal bonds are in one plane then bridging bonds are in perpendicular plane.

3NaBH4+4BF3⟶ether450 K3NaBF4+2B2H6

Therefore we can say that only statement (C) and (D) are correct.

In case of BF3 (2P-2P back bonding) while in BCl3(2P-3P),BBr3(2P-4P) and BI3(2P-5P) back bonding. So extent of back bonding is maximum in 2P-2P

subject: Chemistry
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
For Statement I:
Among N, As, Sb, P, the electronegativity order is N > P > As > Sb. Thus X = N (most electronegative) and Y = Sb (least electronegative). $N_2O_3$ is acidic (forms HNO₂ with water) and $Sb_2O_3$ is amphoteric (reacts with both acids and bases).
Statement I is true.
For Statement II:
While $BCl_3$ is covalent and hydrolyzes in water to form $B(OH)_3$, the hydrolysis products are incorrect. In neutral aqueous medium, $B(OH)_3$ is formed. The tetrahedral $[B(OH)_4]^-$ forms only in basic conditions, and boron does not form $[B(H_2O)_6]^{3+}$ (boron is not a typical transition metal).
Statement II is false.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The stability of oxidation states in Group 14 is governed by the inert pair effect, which increases down the group.
For Lead ($Pb$), the $+2$ oxidation state is more stable than the $+4$ state ($Pb^{2+} > Pb^{4+}$).
In the reaction $PbO_2 + Pb \rightarrow 2PbO$, $Pb^{4+}$ is being reduced to $Pb^{2+}$ and $Pb^0$ is being oxidized to $Pb^{2+}$. Since the product contains the more stable oxidation state, the reaction is spontaneous, meaning $\Delta_r G^\circ(1) < 0$.
For Tin ($Sn$), the $+4$ oxidation state is more stable than the $+2$ state ($Sn^{4+} > Sn^{2+}$).
In the reaction $SnO_2 + Sn \rightarrow 2SnO$, the stable $Sn^{4+}$ is being converted into the less stable $Sn^{2+}$. This process is non-spontaneous, meaning $\Delta_r G^\circ(2) > 0$.
Thus, the correct set is $\Delta_r G^\circ(1) < 0$ and $\Delta_r G^\circ(2) > 0$.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Among $\mathrm{Al}, \mathrm{In}, \mathrm{Tl}, \mathrm{Ge}, \mathrm{Sn}, \mathrm{Pb}$, the metal having highest $\mathrm{IE}_1$ is Ge and lowest $\mathrm{IE}_1$ is In .
Most stable oxidation state of Ge is +4 and In is +3 .

subject: Chemistry
options: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
Statement I: Checking amphoteric nature of oxide pairs:
$[SiO_2, CO_2]$: Both acidic (non-metal oxides)
$[SnO, SnO_2]$: Both amphoteric
$[PbO, PbO_2]$: $PbO$ is amphoteric but $PbO_2$ is predominantly acidic/oxidizing
$[GeO, GeO_2]$: Both amphoteric (Ge is metalloid)
2 pairs have both oxides amphoteric. Statement I is true.
Statement II: $BF_3$ has B with 6 valence electrons (electron deficient), acts as Lewis acid, forms $BF_3 \cdot NH_3$ adduct, has $sp^2$ hybridization giving trigonal planar geometry. Statement II is true.
